message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   Complete a full architectural refactor of the OmegA Vercel app using a production-grade multi-component pattern. The user's explicit instruction was: "option a use this code and plug my neon db in the backend or whatever" — meaning implement the reference architecture code provided (with hooks and components) and integrate it with the existing Neon PostgreSQL database (omega_chat_messages, omega_sessions tables) and existing API endpoints (/api/chat, /api/sessions, /api/stt, /api/tts). The refactor should move away from the current simple page.tsx to a component-driven architecture with proper state management, voice support, responsive design, and modal overlays.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Custom React hooks (useOmegaChat, useVoiceEngine, useIsMobile) for encapsulated state and business logic
   - Streaming API integration with AbortController for cancellation and real-time message handling
   - Web Speech API wrapper for speech-to-text (STT) with interim transcript support
   - Component composition pattern with 3-column layout: sidebar | main area | telemetry panel
   - Responsive mobile detection using window resize listener (768px breakpoint)
   - Session-based conversation management with Neon DB persistence
   - State machines: system state (idle, thinking, followup, error) and voice state (idle, listening, thinking, followup, error)
   - Modal overlays for voice mode, settings, system state debugging, and action sheets
   - ReadableStream handling for streaming responses from /api/chat endpoint
   - Tailwind CSS for styling with responsive classes

3. Files and Code Sections:

   **Hooks Created (in prior session):**
   
   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/hooks/useOmegaChat.ts`
     - Manages chat state: conversations (array), activeConvoId (string), messages (array), systemState ('idle'|'thinking'|'followup'|'error'), streaming (boolean), error (string)
     - Integrates with `/api/sessions` endpoint (GET to list sessions, POST to create, DELETE to delete)
     - Integrates with `/api/chat` endpoint for streaming message responses
     - Implements sendMessage method that handles streaming response via ReadableStream, uses AbortController for cancellation
     - Creates OmegaMessage objects with properties: id, sessionId, role ('user'|'assistant'), content, createdAt
     - Provides public methods: sendMessage(sessionId, content), createConversation(title), deleteConversation(id), switchConversation(id), retry(messageId), clearError()
     - On component mount, fetches all sessions from /api/sessions

   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/hooks/useVoiceEngine.ts`
     - Web Speech API wrapper for speech-to-text functionality
     - Manages VoiceState: 'idle' | 'listening' | 'thinking' | 'followup' | 'error'
     - Tracks transcript (final user input) and interimTranscript (partial results while listening)
     - Implements audio context and analyser for real-time volume monitoring
     - Settings object structure: { language: string (default 'en-US'), volume: number (0-1), interruptible: boolean }
     - Provides methods: startListening(), stopListening(), exitVoiceMode(), interruptSpeech(), updateSettings(partial settings)
     - Logs confidence tracking (warns if confidence < 0.5)
     - Callbacks for transcript updates and state changes

   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/hooks/useIsMobile.ts`
     - Simple responsive detection hook using window resize listener
     - Default breakpoint: 768px
     - Returns boolean isMobile property

   **Components Created (in prior session):**
   
   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/OmegaHeader.tsx`
     - Top navigation header with title and subtitle
     - Menu button (hamburger icon) hidden on desktop (md breakpoint and above)
     - Settings button
     - Responsive design

   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/OmegaMessage.tsx`
     - Displays individual messages with role-based styling
     - User messages: blue background
     - Assistant messages: gray background
     - Copy button on assistant messages for text copying
     - Timestamp display
     - Whitespace preservation and word-breaking for code/formatted text

   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/ChatView.tsx`
     - Scrollable message container with auto-scroll to bottom on new messages
     - Maps over messages array and renders OmegaMessage components
     - Shows "Start a conversation" placeholder when empty
     - Thinking indicator (animated bouncing dots) when systemState === 'thinking'
     - Error banner displayed when systemState === 'error'

   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/ChatComposer.tsx`
     - Textarea input with keyboard support (Shift+Enter for newline, Enter to send)
     - Displays heardText (interim voice transcript) above input field in real-time
     - Send button (disabled while streaming or if text is empty)
     - Voice toggle button (green when ready, red when recording)
     - Disabled state when streaming is active

   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/ConversationSidebar.tsx`
     - Left sidebar with "+ New Chat" button
     - List of conversations with date-based title formatting
     - Active conversation highlighting
     - Hover-to-delete functionality with confirmation
     - Collapsible (width-0 when isOpen === false)

   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/TelemetryPanel.tsx`
     - Right sidebar showing real-time system state monitoring
     - Displays: State (color-coded: thinking=yellow, error=red, followup=green), Streaming status (with pulsing indicator), Message count, Session ID snippet (first 12 chars)
     - Footer with "OmegA v2.0 Powered by Neon DB"
     - Collapsible (width-0 when isOpen === false)

   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/VoiceMode.tsx`
     - Full-screen modal overlay (z-50) shown during active voice interaction
     - Animated orb/microphone icon with pulsing animation
     - State-based border colors: blue (listening), yellow (thinking), green (followup), red (error)
     - Displays transcript + interimTranscript in white box
     - Interrupt and Stop action buttons
     - Props: isActive, state, transcript, interimTranscript, onStop, onInterrupt

   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/SystemStateDrawer.tsx`
     - Right-side drawer modal for debugging system state
     - Displays arbitrary state object as JSON with syntax highlighting
     - Sticky close button at top
     - Scrollable content area

   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/SettingsModal.tsx`
     - Modal dialog for voice engine settings configuration
     - Language dropdown with options: en-US, en-GB, es-ES, fr-FR, de-DE
     - Volume slider (0-1 range, displays as 0-100%)
     - Interruptible checkbox toggle
     - Save and Cancel buttons
     - Local state management with useState for unsaved changes

   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/ActionSheet.tsx`
     - Bottom sheet action menu component (mobile-style interaction)
     - Maps array of Action objects with properties: id, label, icon, destructive, onClick
     - Destructive actions displayed in red text
     - Cancel button at bottom
     - Closes automatically after action is clicked

   **Current Discovered File:**
   
   - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/app/page.tsx`
     - Current simple implementation with useVoice hook
     - Contains basic Home component with "OmegA V2V" title and voice recording button
     - This file needs to be replaced or significantly refactored with the new Index component

4. Errors and fixes:
   - In the prior session, there were two notable errors that were corrected:
     1. **Family data ingestion error**: I incorrectly updated existing profiles (Jay, Suzanne, Trenda) when the user only wanted new profiles created for extended family (Randi, James, Amy, Michael). User feedback: "you were only supposed to lookup and build profiles for my extended family like randi, james, amy, michael, etc." and "i just told you to finish the research on the extended family that  was in the middle of doing, i.e. the randi,amy,james shit". Fix: Reverted the incorrect updates to existing profiles.
     2. **State enum mismatch**: Used 'processing' instead of 'thinking' in the state machine. Fix: Changed all references to use 'thinking' to match the design patterns.
   - In the current session: No errors encountered yet. The initial Glob and Read operations were successful in locating the current page.tsx file.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Identified 7 UI/UX gaps in the current tranquility implementation through design research comparison
   - Unified visual language by creating shared OmegaMessage and animated orb components
   - Added voice transcript confirmation by surfacing heardText (interim transcript) in ChatComposer for real-time feedback
   - Aligned state vocabulary to match established design patterns (idle, thinking, followup, error)
   - Executed parallel agent work in the prior session to accelerate UI improvements and component generation
   - In the current session: began systematic implementation of the reference architecture by reading component files to understand their structure before integration

6. All user messages:
   - "option a use this code and plug my neon db in the backend or whatever" — explicit instruction to use the reference architecture and integrate with Neon
   - Various prior session messages requesting the refactor, handling family data research, and providing the reference architecture design
   - Messages correcting data ingestion errors and state vocabulary mismatches
   - Current interruption: "CRITICAL: Respond with TEXT ONLY" — asking for a comprehensive summary

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Replace `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/app/page.tsx` with new Index component orchestrating all hooks and components
   - Create the main Index/page component that:
     * Uses useOmegaChat hook for all chat state management
     * Uses useVoiceEngine hook for voice input handling
     * Uses useIsMobile hook for responsive layout decisions
     * Implements the 3-column layout: ConversationSidebar | (ChatView + ChatComposer) | TelemetryPanel
     * Wires up all modal/drawer toggles (SettingsModal, SystemStateDrawer, ActionSheet, VoiceMode)
     * Handles the voice transcript callback flow (heardText from useVoiceEngine → sendMessage via useOmegaChat)
     * Manages responsive behavior (collapsing sidebar/telemetry on mobile, showing modals)
   - Test full integration end-to-end to ensure all hooks and components work together
   - Deploy the refactored app to Vercel

8. Current Work:
   In the current session, I began the integration phase by:
   - Attempting to locate the tranquility directory (which does not exist)
   - Using Glob to search for page.tsx files and found the main one at `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/app/page.tsx`
   - Reading the current page.tsx file which contains a simple Home component with basic voice button functionality
   - Planning to read the existing component files (ActionSheet.tsx, SettingsModal.tsx, SystemStateDrawer.tsx, VoiceMode.tsx, TelemetryPanel.tsx) to understand their full structure before integration
   
   The work was interrupted before completing any edits. The next immediate step was to read the component files that were created in the prior session to fully understand their implementation details before wiring them into the main page.tsx.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Create the main Index component at `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/app/page.tsx` that orchestrates all the hooks (useOmegaChat, useVoiceEngine, useIsMobile) and components (OmegaHeader, ChatView, ChatComposer, ConversationSidebar, TelemetryPanel, VoiceMode, SettingsModal, SystemStateDrawer, ActionSheet) into a unified application. This component should:
   
   - Initialize useOmegaChat for chat state and session management
   - Initialize useVoiceEngine for voice input with transcript handling
   - Initialize useIsMobile for responsive layout logic
   - Implement state for modal visibility (settingsOpen, stateDrawerOpen, actionSheetOpen)
   - Build the 3-column responsive layout with proper sidebar/telemetry collapsing on mobile
   - Wire the voice transcript callback (when voice transcript is received, automatically call chat.sendMessage)
   - Connect all button handlers to their respective modal toggles
   - Handle API responses and error states from chat operations
   
   This directly continues the user's explicit request: "option a use this code and plug my neon db in the backend or whatever" by completing the integration of all the previously created hooks and components into a functional, cohesive application that bridges the frontend UI with the Neon database backend.

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /home/mega/.claude/projects/-home-mega/878d4e6a-76ea-45d1-a1ae-d183ba23dcaa.jsonl

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /home/mega/.claude/skills/agent-os

# Agent OS — The gAIng Command Layer

The shared bundle at `/home/mega/claude-skill-bundle/skills` is your **Agent OS** — a 165-skill operating system for the entire crew. This skill is the control center.

## The Four Core Operations

### 1. /start-mission — Launch a Complex Objective
**Skill:** `multi-agent-orchestrator`

| Agent | Role | Best For |
|---|---|---|
| **Claude** | Lead Architect + Coordination | Plans, logs, reviews, synthesis |
| **** | Strategic Analysis + DB | SQL tuning, deep research, performance |
| **Codex** | Architecture + Security | Codebase mapping, security audits, cross-repo |
| **Aider** | Surgical Implementation | File editing, bug fixes, test writing |

**Workflow:**
1. Decompose the goal into 3–5 subtasks with explicit dependencies
2. Assign each subtask to the best-fit agent
3. Use `forward` skill to hand off with full context (file paths, errors, specs)
4. Sync via `shared-state-sync` after each major step
5. Log completion to `~/NEXUS/ERGON.md` with `log` skill

**Example decomposition:**
```
Goal: "Build the OmegA memory retrieval API"
├── : Audit Neon schema, propose query plan
├── Aider: Implement the endpoint
├── Codex: Security review + threat model
└── Claude: Wire up, review PR, log to ERGON.md
```

---

### 2. /sync — Catch Up After an Agent Switch
**Skill:** `shared-state-sync`

**Every session should start with:**
```
1. Read last 20 lines of ~/NEXUS/ERGON.md
2. Run `git status` to catch other agents' uncommitted changes
3. Synthesize: "What was done? What's next? Any conflicts?"
4. Before editing a file — check ERGON.md for active locks
```

**ERGON.md log format:**
```
[2026-03-26 14:30] [CLAUDE] Fixed auth middleware. Status: DONE
[2026-03-26 14:45] [] Auditing Neon slow queries. Status: IN_PROGRESS
```

---

### 3. /health — Diagnose the Mesh
**Skill:** `agent-mesh-health-check`

**Checklist:**
- [ ] `secure-agent-mesh` — trust boundaries nominal
- [ ] `telegram-agent-ops` — Telegram webhooks live
- [ ] ERGON.md — readable and writable
- [ ] `skills-manifest.json` — 165 skills present
- [ ] All agents: responding to forwarded tasks

**Recovery:** If an agent is stuck → `-repo-diagnose` → `skill-bootstrapper` to re-sync.

---

### 4. /bootstrap — Install Everything on a New Agent
**Skill:** `skill-bootstrapper`

Point any new agent at:
```
/home/mega/claude-skill-bundle/skills/universal/skill-bootstrapper/SKILL.md
```
Say: *"Read the bootstrapper and install all 165 skills for your platform."*

The bootstrapper translates the universal SKILL.md format into whatever the target agent needs.

---

## The Power Move: Skill Chaining

**When uncertain which skill to use, ask:**
> "Based on my goal, chain 3 skills from the bundle."

**Algorithm:**
1. Read `skills-manifest.json` — identify skills by keyword match on goal
2. Map dependencies (research → plan → implement → verify)
3. Return a named chain with agent assignments

**Proven Chains:**

| Goal | Chain |
|---|---|
| Understand unfamiliar repo | `repo-cartographer` → `security-threat-model` → `spec-to-code-auditor` |
| Ship a new feature | `autonomous-researcher` → `forward` (Aider) → `eval-forge` |
| Security audit | `repo-cartographer` → `security-ownership-map` → `security-threat-model` |
| Fix failing CI | `gh-fix-ci` → `webapp-testing` → `review-pr` |
| Deploy OmegA | `sovereign-deployer` → `deploy-to-vercel` → `teleodynamics-observer` |
| DB performance | `-repo-cartographer` →  `eval-forge` → `neon-postgres` |
| Build MCP server | `mcp-builder` → `mcp-integration` → `hook-development` |
| Red-team OmegA | `adversarial-evaluator` → `eval-forge` → `memory-utility-maximizer` |
| Design → Code | `figma` → `figma-implement-design` → `vercel-react-best-practices` |
| New skill creation | `skill-architect` → `skill-development` → `claude-md-improver` |

---

## Skill Directory: The Full 67-Skill Claude Set

### Meta / Coordination
`agent-os` · `multi-agent-orchestrator` · `shared-state-sync` · `agent-mesh-health-check` · `skill-bootstrapper` · `forward` · `task` · `briefing` · `wake-up` · `gains` · `log`

### Codebase Intelligence
`repo-cartographer` · `-repo-cartographer` · `spec-to-code-auditor` · `-repo-diagnose` · `-repo-cleanup` · `-repo-finalize` · `shadow-workflow-gen`

### Security
`security-threat-model` · `security-best-practices` · `security-ownership-map` · `adversarial-evaluator` · `secure-agent-mesh`

### OmegA-Specific
`omega-sid-orchestrator` · `omega-workflow-orchestrator` · `omega-sovereign-ui` · `-omega-sovereign-ops` · `sovereign-deployer` · `teleodynamics-observer` · `provider-router-simulator` · `memory-utility-maximizer` · `synergistic-chain-optimizer`

### Frontend / UI
`software-frontend` · `senior-frontend` · `frontend` · `vercel-react-best-practices` · `vercel-react-native-skills` · `vercel-composition-patterns` · `figma` · `figma-implement-design` · `omega-sovereign-ui`

### Backend / API
`backend-development` · `backend-dev-guidelines` · `fastapi-python` · `fastapi-templates` · `neon-postgres` · `mcp-builder` · `mcp-integration`

### DevOps / Deploy
`deploy-to-vercel` · `gh-fix-ci` · `gh-address-comments` · `review-pr` · `commit` · `create-pr` · `hook-development`

### Research / Eval / Agents
`autonomous-researcher` · `eval-forge` · `prompt-forge` · `skill-architect` · `skill-development` · `one-block-builder`

### Integrations
`telegram-agent-ops` · `sentry` · `linear` · `notion-knowledge-capture` · `notion-research-documentation` · `claude-api` · `claude-md-improver` · `screenshot` · `pdf` · `doc` · `playwright` · `webapp-testing`

---

## Key Paths

| Resource | Path |
|---|---|
| The Block (shared log) | `~/NEXUS/ERGON.md` |
| Skill Bundle | `/home/mega/claude-skill-bundle/skills/` |
| Skill Manifest | `/home/mega/claude-skill-bundle/skills/skills-manifest.json` |
| Skill Index | `/home/mega/claude-skill-bundle/skills/INDEX.md` |
| Claude Skills | `/home/mega/.claude/skills/` |
| OmegA Repo | `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CANON/OmegA-Architecture` |
| Vault | `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/VAULT/one-true.env` |
| Neon DB | `public.omega_memory_entries` (26k+ entries) |

---

## Rules

1. **Always sync first** — read ERGON.md before starting any multi-agent work
2. **Always log completions** — use `log` skill after every major step
3. **Chain don't repeat** — if a skill exists for it, use it; don't reinvent inline
4. **Pass full context on handoff** — file paths, error messages, current state
5. **The Block is truth** — ERGON.md wins over memory when there's a conflict

I'll continue integrating the architecture by reading the remaining components and then wiring them into the main page.tsx.

Let me find and read all the remaining components:

message.content:
CRITICAL: Respond with TEXT ONLY. Do NOT call any tools.

- Do NOT use Read, Bash, Grep, Glob, Edit, Write, or ANY other tool.
- You already have all the context you need in the conversation above.
- Tool calls will be REJECTED and will waste your only turn — you will fail the task.
- Your entire response must be plain text: an <analysis> block followed by a <summary> block.

Your task is to create a detailed summary of the conversation so far, paying close attention to the user's explicit requests and your previous actions.
This summary should be thorough in capturing technical details, code patterns, and architectural decisions that would be essential for continuing development work without losing context.

Before providing your final summary, wrap your analysis in <analysis> tags to organize your thoughts and ensure you've covered all necessary points. In your analysis process:

1. Chronologically analyze each message and section of the conversation. For each section thoroughly identify:
   - The user's explicit requests and intents
   - Your approach to addressing the user's requests
   - Key decisions, technical concepts and code patterns
   - Specific details like:
     - file names
     - full code snippets
     - function signatures
     - file edits
   - Errors that you ran into and how you fixed them
   - Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
2. Double-check for technical accuracy and completeness, addressing each required element thoroughly.

Your summary should include the following sections:

1. Primary Request and Intent: Capture all of the user's explicit requests and intents in detail
2. Key Technical Concepts: List all important technical concepts, technologies, and frameworks discussed.
3. Files and Code Sections: Enumerate specific files and code sections examined, modified, or created. Pay special attention to the most recent messages and include full code snippets where applicable and include a summary of why this file read or edit is important.
4. Errors and fixes: List all errors that you ran into, and how you fixed them. Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
5. Problem Solving: Document problems solved and any ongoing troubleshooting efforts.
6. All user messages: List ALL user messages that are not tool results. These are critical for understanding the users' feedback and changing intent.
7. Pending Tasks: Outline any pending tasks that you have explicitly been asked to work on.
8. Current Work: Describe in detail precisely what was being worked on immediately before this summary request, paying special attention to the most recent messages from both user and assistant. Include file names and code snippets where applicable.
9. Optional Next Step: List the next step that you will take that is related to the most recent work you were doing. IMPORTANT: ensure that this step is DIRECTLY in line with the user's most recent explicit requests, and the task you were working on immediately before this summary request. If your last task was concluded, then only list next steps if they are explicitly in line with the users request. Do not start on tangential requests or really old requests that were already completed without confirming with the user first.
                       If there is a next step, include direct quotes from the most recent conversation showing exactly what task you were working on and where you left off. This should be verbatim to ensure there's no drift in task interpretation.

Here's an example of how your output should be structured:

<example>

[Your thought process, ensuring all points are covered thoroughly and accurately]
</analysis>

   [Detailed description]

</summary>
</example>

Please provide your summary based on the conversation so far, following this structure and ensuring precision and thoroughness in your response. 

There may be additional summarization instructions provided in the included context. If so, remember to follow these instructions when creating the above summary. Examples of instructions include:
<example>
## Compact Instructions
When summarizing the conversation focus on typescript code changes and also remember the mistakes you made and how you fixed them.

This is a full architectural refactor of the OmegA Vercel app to move from a simple page.tsx to a production-grade multi-component pattern with proper state management, voice support, responsive design, and modal overlays. 2. **Key Technical Concepts:** - Custom React hooks for encapsulated state and business logic (useOmegaChat, useVoiceEngine, useIsMobile) - Streaming API integration with AbortController for real-time message handling - Web Speech API wrapper for speech-to-text with interim transcript support - Component composition pattern with 3-column responsive layout - Session-based conversation management with Neon DB persistence - State machines: system state (idle, thinking, followup, error) and voice state (idle, listening, thinking, followup, error) - Modal overlays and responsive behavior with window resize listeners - ReadableStream handling for streaming responses from /api/chat endpoint - Tailwind CSS responsive design with 768px breakpoint for mobile detection 3. **Files and Code Sections:** **Components (All Read in This Session):** - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/OmegaHeader.tsx` — Header with hamburger menu and settings button, responsive design - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/OmegaMessage.tsx` — Individual message display with role-based styling (blue for user, gray for assistant) and copy functionality - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/ChatView.tsx` — Scrollable message container with auto-scroll, thinking indicator, and error banner - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/ChatComposer.tsx` — Textarea input with voice transcript display and Send/Voice buttons - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/ConversationSidebar.tsx` — Left sidebar with conversation list, new chat button, and delete functionality - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/TelemetryPanel.tsx` — Right sidebar showing system state, streaming status, message count, session ID - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/VoiceMode.tsx` — Full-screen modal during voice interaction with animated orb and transcript display - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/SystemStateDrawer.tsx` — Right-side drawer for debugging system state as JSON - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/SettingsModal.tsx` — Settings dialog with language dropdown, volume slider, interruptible checkbox - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/components/omega/ActionSheet.tsx` — Bottom sheet action menu with destructive action styling **Hooks (All Read in This Session):** - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/hooks/useOmegaChat.ts` - Core chat state management hook - Manages: conversations (array), activeConvoId (string), messages (array), systemState ('idle'|'thinking'|'followup'|'error'), streaming (boolean), error (string) - Key interfaces: OmegaMessage, OmegaSession, OmegaChatState - Public methods: sendMessage(text), createConversation(), deleteConversation(id), switchConversation(id), retry(messageId), clearError() - Integration with /api/sessions for session management and /api/chat for streaming responses - Uses AbortController for cancellation, ReadableStream for real-time message handling - Automatically loads sessions on mount and manages message streaming with incremental updates - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/hooks/useVoiceEngine.ts` - Web Speech API wrapper for speech-to-text - State tracking: voiceState ('idle'|'listening'|'thinking'|'followup'|'error'), transcript, interimTranscript, isListening - Settings object: language (default 'en-US'), volume (0-1), interruptible (boolean) - Public methods: startListening(), stopListening(), exitVoiceMode(), interruptSpeech(), updateSettings() - Confidence tracking with console warnings for low confidence (<0.5) - Audio context and analyser for volume monitoring - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/hooks/useIsMobile.ts` - Simple responsive detection hook - Default breakpoint: 768px - Returns boolean isMobile property - Uses window resize listener **Main Integration File (Edited in This Session):** - `/home/mega/OMEGA_WORKSPACE/CODE/omega-web/omega-web/src/app/page.tsx` — REPLACED with comprehensive Index component **The New Index Component Implementation:** ```typescript 'use client'; import React, { useEffect, useState } from 'react'; import { useOmegaChat } from '../hooks/useOmegaChat'; import { useVoiceEngine } from '../hooks/useVoiceEngine'; import { useIsMobile } from '../hooks/useIsMobile'; import { OmegaHeader } from '../components/omega/OmegaHeader'; import { ChatView } from '../components/omega/ChatView'; import { ChatComposer } from '../components/omega/ChatComposer'; import { ConversationSidebar } from '../components/omega/ConversationSidebar'; import { TelemetryPanel } from '../components/omega/TelemetryPanel'; import { VoiceMode } from '../components/omega/VoiceMode'; import { SettingsModal } from '../components/omega/SettingsModal'; import { SystemStateDrawer } from '../components/omega/SystemStateDrawer'; export default function Index() { const chat = useOmegaChat(); const voice = useVoiceEngine(); const isMobile = useIsMobile(); // Modal state management const [settingsOpen, setSettingsOpen] = useState(false); const [stateDrawerOpen, setStateDrawerOpen] = useState(false); const [sidebarOpen, setSidebarOpen] = useState(!isMobile); const [telemetryOpen, setTelemetryOpen] = useState(!isMobile); // Voice mode tracking const voiceModeActive = voice.isListening || voice.voiceState !== 'idle'; // Auto-send transcript when voice listening completes useEffect(() => { if ( voice.transcript && voice.voiceState === 'idle' && !voice.isListening ) { const finalText = voice.transcript.trim(); if (finalText) { chat.sendMessage(finalText); voice.exitVoiceMode(); } } }, [voice.voiceState, voice.isListening, voice.transcript]); // Event handlers for all interactions const handleVoiceStart = () => voice.startListening(); const handleVoiceStop = () => voice.stopListening(); const handleVoiceInterrupt = () => voice.interruptSpeech(); const handleSendMessage = (text: string) => chat.sendMessage(text); const handleNewConversation = async () => await chat.createConversation(); const handleSelectConversation = async (id: string) => await chat.switchConversation(id); const handleDeleteConversation = async (id: string) => { if (confirm('Delete this conversation?')) { await chat.deleteConversation(id); } }; const handleSettingsChange = (settings: any) => { voice.updateSettings(settings); setSettingsOpen(false); }; // Responsive sidebar behavior useEffect(() => { setSidebarOpen(!isMobile); setTelemetryOpen(!isMobile); }, [isMobile]); return (
{/* 3-Column Layout */} {/* Main Content: Header + ChatView + ChatComposer */}
setSidebarOpen(!sidebarOpen)} onSettingsClick={() => setSettingsOpen(true)} />
{/* Right Telemetry Panel */} {/* All Modals and Overlays */} setSettingsOpen(false)} settings={voice.settings} onSettingsChange={handleSettingsChange} /> setStateDrawerOpen(false)} state={{ chatState: chat.systemState, voiceState: voice.voiceState, activeSession: chat.activeConvoId, messageCount: chat.messages.length, streaming: chat.streaming, }} /> {/* Mobile-only telemetry toggle */} {isMobile && ( )}
); } ``` **Why This File Edit is Important:** This is the integration point that orchestrates the entire application. The old page.tsx was a simple 4-line component with just a voice button.
